mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and moderate inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series benchmark, or quarterly returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term market returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal reverses during liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth exceeding 200,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations for positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the predictive alpha vanishes when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending, directly reducing corporate revenue forecasts and driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly returns remain positive in >60% of instances following a >1.5% retail sales drop, or the signal is fully subsumed by monetary policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation/low-rate environments, or market pricing of rate hikes fails to materialize in bond yields.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter revisions to real GDP growth reflect underlying economic resilience, providing a baseline positive drift for equity markets over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative mean following positive GDP revisions, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from random noise over a 5-year sample.
